old_claim.note: Early Superconductors is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: Superconducting magnets are important to modern magnetic-confinement fusion systems such as ITER, but early fusion research predated them.

source_shape.source_locator: DOE fusion nuclear science and technology page describing ITER central solenoid superconducting cables and fusion-system development.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports superconductors as important modern fusion magnet infrastructure rather than the historical origin of fusion research.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The edge is now scaling infrastructure for magnetic-confinement systems, preserving its relevance without implying early fusion research historically descended from superconductivity.

ontology_before: The old edge represented superconductors as a historical predecessor for all fusion research.
ontology_after: The edge is constrained to commercial/scaling dependency for modern fusion magnet systems.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant added in 2026-06-07-energy-edge-source-scope.json to lock the corrected Energy Systems edge semantics.
would_reject_if: A more specific primary or textbook source showed the original dependency type was globally required for the scoped technology.
The dependent node were narrowed to a later industrial product where the old prerequisite is truly mandatory.
A future source-check splits the node and moves this edge onto a more precise downstream technology.
short_reason: Superconductors scale modern fusion magnets but did not precede fusion research as a whole.
